message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

  - LLM timeout + retry + 503

#영역현재 상태4h 안에 박는 것시간1Persistencedict[UUID, X] globalSQLite + SQLModel(async) — Repository protocol 인터페이스로 store 추상화. Depends(get_repo)60분2Async LLMclient.messages.create 동기 호출AsyncAnthropic + async def 라우트. LLM 호출 30s 동안 worker 안 막음20분3Dependency Injectionmodule global importDepends(get_store), Depends(get_llm), Depends(get_settings). test는 app.dependency_overrides15분4Settingsos.getenv() 산재pydantic-settings.BaseSettings 1개 클래스. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, MODEL, USE_VERTEX, DB_URL 한 곳에10분5Centralized exception handlerroutes 마다 try/except@app.exception_handler(NotFoundError) 1개 등록. routes 절반으로 줄음5분

🟠 Tier 2 — production 운영에서 매번 깨짐 (operational)
#영역박는 것왜6AuthenticationHTTPBearer 의존성 + session.owner_id + 라우트별 ownership 검증현재 누구나 누구 session 검사 가능7Structured logging + trace_id 전파structlog + Depends(get_logger). trace_id가 모든 로그에 박힘디버깅 불가능한 production은 production 아님8OpenTelemetryopentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi. 라우트/LLM 호출/DB query spanDH JD 직격 키워드 (OTEL/GDP)9Rate limitingslowapi middleware + per-API-key bucket토큰 비용 폭탄 방지10Idempotency keysIdempotency-Key 헤더 → 24h Redis 캐시. 동일 키 재요청 시 캐시 반환LLM 호출은 비싸고 비결정적. retry-safe 필수11Circuit breaker for LLMtenacity + 5xx 비율 임계 → 30초 fail-fast 503Anthropic 장애 시 cascading failure 방지12Audit log별도 테이블 audit_events(actor, action, resource, payload, timestamp, trace_id). 모든 LLM 호출의 prompt+response 저장

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/slides/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → FigJam file, use get_figjam; pass the original board URL as figjamUrl when available
- figma.com/slides/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → Figma Slides file

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the t… [truncated]

attachment.content:
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- diff-review: Explain an implementation diff and produce a prioritized code review checklist. Use when the user asks for /diff-review, local code review, diff explanation, or what reviewers should inspect first.
- review: Multi-persona code review — Linus, Fowler, and Vogels each review a PR and have a discussion round. Uses local Claude token. Run as /review <PR_NUMBER>.
- fastapi: FastAPI best practices — project structure, route patterns, Pydantic v2, dependency injection, settings, error handling. References: fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template, benavlabs/FastAPI-boilerplate, zhanymkanov/fastapi-best-practices, fastapi-practices/fastapi_best_architecture.
- changelog-generator: Automatically creates user-facing changelogs from git commits by analyzing commit history, categorizing changes, and transforming technical commits into clear, customer-friendly release notes. Turns hours of manual changelog writing into minutes of automated generation.
- webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
- feature: Interactive feature workflow — gather requirements interactively (description, API list, branch name, context), then run design → implement → PR in sequence. Use when the user wants to build a new feature end-to-end.
- plan-for-codex: Analyze the user's request and write a PLAN.md at the worktree root that Codex (running in another tab on the same worktree) can read and implement. Do not modify any code yourself — only produce the plan. Use when the user wants Claude to design and Codex to implement.
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- assignment: End-to-end assignment workflow — loads tech stack constraints → /think (design, 7 stages) → /iterate (Codex implements) → README polish → final gate. Usage: /assignment <assignment description or path>
- diff-review: Diff Review Brief
- iterate: Multi-agent iteration loop with human checkpoints — Pre-build → Builder → Spot-check → Tester → Reviewer → Human. Usage: /iterate <feature description>
- code-review: Multi-axis code review across correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use before merging any change — from yourself, another agent, or a human. Usage: /code-review [PR# | git ref | empty for current diff]
- plan: Design Agent — 3-phase collaborative design with human checkpoints at each stage. Outputs REQUIREMENTS.md + PLAN.md. Usage: /plan
- design: Analyze requirements and create a design spec before implementation. Writes to /tmp/spec.md and waits for user approval.
- think: Design thinking partner — walks alongside you through 7-stage expert planning. Shows the internal monologue of a senior engineer at each stage, then asks sharp questions to sharpen your thinking. Usage: /think <problem or decision>
- assignment: Execute the Agentic IDE take-home assignment playbook
- evaluate: Score a submission against the IC1 Backend 5-axis rubric (25 points). Usage: /evaluate [path-to-submission]
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Build production-ready LLM API with FastAPI

content.text: 이건 future project용 implementation checklist로 memory에 저장해두는 게 제일 좋겠어요. 지금 바로 저장할게요.

1	# Memory Index
2	
3	- [project_dh_case_study.md](project_dh_case_study.md) — DH Agentic IDEs IC1 take-home: case-01-session-backend, execution protocol at ASSIGNMENT_EXECUTION_PROTOCOL.md
4	- [user_profile.md](user_profile.md) — User is a backend engineer applying to DH Agentic IDEs IC1; has MCP/multi-agent experience; uses worktrees + parallel agents in personal workflow

# Memory Index

- [project_dh_case_study.md](project_dh_case_study.md) — DH Agentic IDEs IC1 take-home: case-01-session-backend, execution protocol at ASSIGNMENT_EXECUTION_PROTOCOL.md
- [user_profile.md](user_profile.md) — User is a backend engineer applying to DH Agentic IDEs IC1; has MCP/multi-agent experience; uses worktrees + parallel agents in personal workflow
- [feedback_llm_integration.md](feedback_llm_integration.md) — LLM integration mistakes: always use tool_use (not json.loads), max_tokens≥4096, load_dotenv() in main.py, curl→file not pipe
- [feedback_test_isolation.md](feedback_test_isolation.md) — Tests must force ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="" in conftest.py; never assert exact LLM output counts

input.content:
---
name: FastAPI Production Checklist (Tier 1 & 2)
description: Must-have and operational patterns for every new FastAPI backend project — what to implement and in what order
type: feedback
---

Every new FastAPI backend project needs these before shipping. Tier 1 blocks staging; Tier 2 causes repeated production incidents.

## Tier 1 — staging 불가 (implement first, ~2h total)

1. **Persistence** — SQLite + SQLModel(async) behind a Repository protocol interface. Routes get `Depends(get_repo)` — never touch DB directly. (~60min, unlocks everything else)
2. **Async LLM** — `AsyncAnthropic` + `async def` routes. Never call `client.messages.create` synchronously; it holds the worker for 30s.
3. **Dependency Injection** — `Depends(get_store)`, `Depends(get_llm)`, `Depends(get_settings)`. Tests use `app.dependency_overrides` — no module-level globals.
4. **Settings** — `pydantic-settings.BaseSettings` single class. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MODEL`, `USE_VERTEX`, `DB_URL` all in one place.
5. **Centralized exception handler** — `@app.exception_handler(NotFoundError)` registered in `main.py`. Removes per-route try/except boilerplate.

## Tier 2 — production 운영 (implement before real traffic)

6. **Auth + ownership** — `HTTPBearer` dependency + `session.owner_id == actor` check on every resource route. Anyone can read anyone's session without this.
7. **Structured logging + trace_id** — `structlog` + `Depends(get_logger)`. Every log line gets `trace_id`, `actor`, `session_id`. Undebuggable production = not production.
8. **OpenTelemetry** — `opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi`. Spans on routes, LLM calls, DB queries. DH JD direct keyword (OTEL/GDP).
9. **Rate limiting** — `slowapi` middleware + per-API-key bucket. Prevents token cost explosion.
10. **Idempotency keys (LLM endpoints only)** — `Idempotency-Key` header → SQLite cache (24h). Same key re-request returns cached response. LLM calls are expensive and non-deterministic; retry-safe is mandatory. SQLite is sufficient (no Redis needed for simple cases).
11. **Circuit breaker for LLM** — `tenacity` + 5xx rate threshold → 30s fail-fast 503. Prevents cascading failure during Anthropic outages.
12. **Audit log** — separate `audit_events` table: `(actor, action, resource, payload, timestamp, trace_id)`. Log every LLM call's prompt + response.

**Why:** Tier 1 없으면 staging도 못 띄움. Tier 2 없으면 production에서 매번 같은 문제(비용 폭탄, 디버깅 불가, 보안 구멍)가 반복됨.

**How to apply:** 새 FastAPI 프로젝트 시작할 때 Tier 1 → Tier 2 순서로 checklist처럼 사용. 시간 제약 있으면 Tier 1 완료 후 Tier 2는 우선순위 순서대로.

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/mae/.claude/projects/-Users-mae-Desktop-Develop-AgenticIDE/memory/feedback_fastapi_production_checklist.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

---
name: FastAPI Production Checklist (Tier 1 & 2)
description: Must-have and operational patterns for every new FastAPI backend project — what to implement and in what order
type: feedback
originSessionId: fc5b61d0-d1d8-4305-b0fe-5d0eadf5fe64
---
Every new FastAPI backend project needs these before shipping. Tier 1 blocks staging; Tier 2 causes repeated production incidents.

## Tier 1 — staging 불가 (implement first, ~2h total)

1. **Persistence** — SQLite + SQLModel(async) behind a Repository protocol interface. Routes get `Depends(get_repo)` — never touch DB directly. (~60min, unlocks everything else)
2. **Async LLM** — `AsyncAnthropic` + `async def` routes. Never call `client.messages.create` synchronously; it holds the worker for 30s.
3. **Dependency Injection** — `Depends(get_store)`, `Depends(get_llm)`, `Depends(get_settings)`. Tests use `app.dependency_overrides` — no module-level globals.
4. **Settings** — `pydantic-settings.BaseSettings` single class. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MODEL`, `USE_VERTEX`, `DB_URL` all in one place.
5. **Centralized exception handler** — `@app.exception_handler(NotFoundError)` registered in `main.py`. Removes per-route try/except boilerplate.

## Tier 2 — production 운영 (implement before real traffic)

6. **Auth + ownership** — `HTTPBearer` dependency + `session.owner_id == actor` check on every resource route. Anyone can read anyone's session without this.
7. **Structured logging + trace_id** — `structlog` + `Depends(get_logger)`. Every log line gets `trace_id`, `actor`, `session_id`. Undebuggable production = not production.
8. **OpenTelemetry** — `opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi`. Spans on routes, LLM calls, DB queries. DH JD direct keyword (OTEL/GDP).
9. **Rate limiting** — `slowapi` middleware + per-API-key bucket. Prevents token cost explosion.
10. **Idempotency keys (LLM endpoints only)** — `Idempotency-Key` header → SQLite cache (24h). Same key re-request returns cached response. LLM calls are expensive and non-deterministic; retry-safe is mandatory. SQLite is sufficient (no Redis needed for simple cases).
11. **Circuit breaker for LLM** — `tenacity` + 5xx rate threshold → 30s fail-fast 503. Prevents cascading failure during Anthropic outages.
12. **Audit log** — separate `audit_events` table: `(actor, action, resource, payload, timestamp, trace_id)`. Log every LLM call's prompt + response.

**Why:** Tier 1 없으면 staging도 못 띄움. Tier 2 없으면 production에서 매번 같은 문제(비용 폭탄, 디버깅 불가, 보안 구멍)가 반복됨.

**How to apply:** 새 FastAPI 프로젝트 시작할 때 Tier 1 → Tier 2 순서로 checklist처럼 사용. 시간 제약 있으면 Tier 1 완료 후 Tier 2는 우선순위 순서대로.

- [feedback_fastapi_production_checklist.md](feedback_fastapi_production_checklist.md) — Tier1/Tier2 checklist for every new FastAPI project: persistence→async LLM→DI→settings→exception handler, then auth+ownership, structlog+trace_id, OTEL, rate limit, idempotency (LLM only, SQLite), circuit breaker, audit log

# Memory Index

- [project_dh_case_study.md](project_dh_case_study.md) — DH Agentic IDEs IC1 take-home: case-01-session-backend, execution protocol at ASSIGNMENT_EXECUTION_PROTOCOL.md
- [user_profile.md](user_profile.md) — User is a backend engineer applying to DH Agentic IDEs IC1; has MCP/multi-agent experience; uses worktrees + parallel agents in personal workflow
- [feedback_llm_integration.md](feedback_llm_integration.md) — LLM integration mistakes: always use tool_use (not json.loads), max_tokens≥4096, load_dotenv() in main.py, curl→file not pipe
- [feedback_test_isolation.md](feedback_test_isolation.md) — Tests must force ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="" in conftest.py; never assert exact LLM output counts

+- [feedback_fastapi_production_checklist.md](feedback_fastapi_production_checklist.md) — Tier1/Tier2 checklist for every new FastAPI project: persistence→async LLM→DI→settings→exception handler, then auth+ownership, structlog+trace_id, OTEL, rate limit, idempotency (LLM only, SQLite), circuit breaker, audit log

content.text:
저장 완료. 다음 프로젝트에서 "FastAPI 프로젝트 시작할 때 뭐부터 해야 해?" 라고 물으면 이 체크리스트를 불러올게요.

핵심 포인트 요약:
- **Idempotency**: LLM endpoint에만, SQLite 24h 캐시로 충분 — Redis 불필요
- **ownership 검증**: `session.owner_id == actor` — `HTTPBearer` deps에서 처리
- **trace_id**: `structlog` + `Depends(get_logger)`로 모든 로그에 자동 전파
- **LLM 장애 대응**: `tenacity` circuit breaker → 5xx 임계 초과 시 30s fail-fast 503

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content: Saved a Tier 1/2 FastAPI production checklist to memory for future projects. No active task — ready for next work.
